title: toxicodendron radicans
intro: Toxicodendron radicans, also known as poison ivy, is a deciduous woody vine or shrub native to eastern North America. The plant contains urushiol, an oily sap that causes skin irritation and allergic reactions in many people, but it also has medicinal properties, such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Toxicodendron radicans include its ability to treat skin conditions like eczema and acne due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of Toxicodendron radicans include ointments, salves, and tinctures, which can be used topically to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
